response.justification: Sarah Smith brings roughly 20 years of hands-on engineering breadth and genuine entrepreneurial initiative, but falls well short of what a director-level AI strategy role demands. Her AI exposure is limited to a few months of MCP tooling work starting April 2025 — not the recognized, multi-year track record of building AI-powered products or platforms the role explicitly requires. Cloud architecture experience is minimal (a passing AWS CDK reference at Optilyz), and there is no evidence of strategic customer advisory work, cross-org technical leadership, or the kind of influence-at-scale expected of a principal engineer at this seniority band.
strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — comfortably meets the 15-year floor
Genuine entrepreneurial track record with GISCollective (founder, open-source, real user base) and a pattern of community knowledge-sharing through talks and workshops
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq signals growing AI interest and practical engagement with agentic workflows
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent; no demonstrated history of architecting or shipping AI-powered products at scale, which is the core differentiator for this role
Cloud depth is absent — one CDK mention does not constitute the cloud platform fluency required to advise strategic customers or identify future cloud product direction
Career arc is consistently IC/senior-developer level with no evidence of director-level scope, cross-functional influence, or strategic partnership advisory work
